method.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
method.name: method

path.description: Full API path including version prefix (e.g. /v1/mandates, /health, /federation/v1/register)
path.name: path

api.description: Call any AGLedger API endpoint. Pass-through with zero flag translation — use the exact path and body shape documented in the API (see `agledger discover` or GET /openapi.json).
examples: agledger api POST /v1/mandates -F contractType=ACH-PROC-v1 -F criteria.item_spec=widgets -F criteria.quantity.target=500
agledger api GET /v1/mandates --paginate  # streams all pages as NDJSON

json.description: Force JSON output (default when stdout is piped)
json.name: json

data.description: Request body as a JSON string. For GET/DELETE, used as query parameters.
data.name: data

input.description: Read body JSON from file (or "-" for stdin). Shape matches --data.
input.name: input

field.description: Set a field as key=value. Values are typed: true/false/null, numbers, or JSON for {...}/[...]. Supports nested (a.b.c=v) and array append (arr[]=v). Repeatable.
field.name: field

query.description: Extra query parameters as JSON. Merges over -F fields and --data for GET/DELETE routing.
query.name: query

paginate.description: Follow cursor pagination, stream all results as NDJSON. Requires GET on a paginated route.
paginate.name: paginate

dry-run.description: Show the request that would be sent without calling the API.
dry-run.name: dry-run

auth.description: Check current authentication status. Returns `authenticated: false` when no key is configured (exit 0).

json.description: Force JSON output (default when stdout is piped)
json.name: json

config.description: Inspect or switch between stored profiles in ~/.agledger/config.json
examples: agledger config list                # list all profiles
agledger config get                  # show active profile
agledger config get --profile prod   # show a specific profile
agledger config use prod             # set prod as the active profile
agledger config path                 # print the config file path

json.description: Force JSON output (default when stdout is piped)
